# Vendored charset mappings (Unicode Consortium MICSFT)

Authoritative code-page ↔ Unicode mapping tables, vendored verbatim from the
Unicode Consortium. These are the **primary source** for the device's own
embedded C89 charset tables (`src/charset_tables.c`, generated by
`tools/gen_charset_tables.py`). We bake our own tables rather than relying on
the OS's `MultiByteToWideChar`/`WideCharToMultiByte`, so the mapping must be
deterministic, identical on every host, and auditable against its source.

## Source

Base URL: <https://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/VENDORS/MICSFT/>

- `WINDOWS/CP<n>.TXT` — the Windows ANSI single-byte pages (`cp1250`–`cp1258`,
  `cp874`) and the four CJK DBCS pages (`cp932`/`936`/`949`/`950`).
- `PC/CP<n>.TXT` — the OEM/DOS single-byte pages (`cp437`/`737`/`775`/`850`/
  `852`/`855`/`857`/`860`/`861`/`862`/`863`/`864`/`865`/`866`/`869`).

These are the **strict** mappings. We deliberately do **not** use
`WindowsBestFit/` — best-fit substitution (e.g. fullwidth → ASCII homoglyphs)
is a path-confusion footgun the device rejects: an unrepresentable code point is
a strict reject, never a silent look-alike.

Format (all files): three tab-separated columns, `0x<byte(s)>` `0x<codepoint>`
`#<name>`. In the DBCS files a single-byte lead is marked `#DBCS LEAD BYTE` with
an empty codepoint column; an unmapped position is `#UNDEFINED` (decodes to
U+FFFD). A four-hex-digit first column (`0x<lead><trail>`) is a double-byte
sequence.

## Integrity

`SHA256SUMS` records the upstream hash of every vendored file. To re-verify
against the live source (or to refresh):

    cd vendor/charset-mappings
    sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS          # verify the committed copies
    # refresh from upstream + re-verify:
    while read -r _ path; do
      curl -sS -o "$path" "https://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/VENDORS/MICSFT/$path"
    done < SHA256SUMS
    sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS

## cp858 — the one derived page (not in MICSFT)

The Unicode Consortium's `MICSFT/PC/` set does **not** include `CP858.TXT`
(it returns HTTP 404). Code page 858 is, by definition, code page 850 with the
Euro sign added: byte `0xD5`, which is `U+0131` (LATIN SMALL LETTER DOTLESS I)
in cp850, becomes `U+20AC` (EURO SIGN) in cp858. Every other position is
identical to cp850.

The generator therefore derives cp858 from the vendored `PC/CP850.TXT` by
applying that single documented override (`0xD5 → U+20AC`). This is the only
table not taken byte-for-byte from a vendored MICSFT file; the derivation is a
one-line, independently verifiable delta (`diff` cp858 against cp850 yields
exactly position `0xD5`) and is recorded in `docs/charset-bijection.md`.
